summary: A small single-storey rectangular modern-style building with a framed raised foundation, continuous horizontal banding of materials, strip windows on all visible sides, and a shallow flat roof with a stone slab parapet. The structure sits centered inside a low white border/planter and is surrounded by leveled dirt ground.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box with inset interior floor (roof visible as flat recessed center)
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long axis (front/back mirror), likely symmetric on both axes visually

materials.name: wood
materials.role: horizontal plank band near top and bottom of walls (likely oak planks)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: parapet/roof trim (slab border around recessed roof)

materials.name: cobblestone
materials.role: middle lower wall textured blocks (accent corners and band)

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: outer low border/planter surrounding building (white smooth blocks)

materials.name: glass
materials.role: strip windows (panes or blocks) centered in wall bands

materials.name: slab_wood
materials.role: possible wooden slab floor visible on roof interior

elements: strip windows (continuous horizontal row, 4 visible panes on long side)
flat recessed roof with slab coping
banded wall layers: bottom wood plank, cobblestone/stone band, white smooth band, upper wood plank band
small corner pilasters of textured stone
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular footprint roughly 11x7 blocks. Raise a 1-block high wooden base band around the perimeter.
Place a cobblestone/stone textured band one block high above the wooden base along the middle of walls; inset strip windows (glass panes) centered in that band on long sides.
Above the textured band place a 1-block high white smooth layer (quartz or similar) then a 1-block high wood plank band at the top of walls.
Add a single-block-high stone slab coping around the roof edge and fill the interior roof area with wood planks set one block lower than the coping to create a recessed flat roof.
Surround the building with a one-block-wide white border/planter (quartz) set flush with ground; ground outside is leveled dirt.
uncertainties: Exact block types for the white smooth layer (assumed quartz) and the upper coping (stone slab vs stone brick slab) are uncertain.
Whether glass units are glass panes or full glass blocks (appearance suggests panes) is not 100% certain.
Precise internal layout, door location, and any interior details are not visible.
Footprint dimensions are estimated from pixel view and top-down proportions; actual block counts may vary by ±1-2 blocks.
